Title: Tenant-aware data model
Description: PostgreSQL/Sequelize schema for orgs, facilities, assets, users, roles, telemetry streams, parsers, monitoring rules, audit events, retention policies; every row tenant-scoped.
Acceptance: Schema+migrations reviewed; all tables tenant-scoped; ERD documented

Title: Tenant isolation enforcement
Description: Enforce the tenant filter at app, every DB query, authz, audit, and test layers; a query missing the filter fails rather than leaks.
Acceptance: Tests prove cross-tenant read/write fail and are logged

Title: Authentication & sessions
Description: Sign-up/in/out, session handling, token issuance/rotation/expiry/revocation, password & access reset, account recovery.
Acceptance: Full login lifecycle works; session tests pass

Title: Modern identity (OIDC/OAuth2/MFA)
Description: Layer OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and multi-factor auth onto A3 where required.
Acceptance: OIDC/OAuth2 flows + MFA demonstrated

Title: Roles & permissions (RBAC)
Description: Role-based access for platform admin, org admin, facility user, viewer, editor, service accounts; least privilege.
Acceptance: Permission matrix enforced and test-covered

Title: Organizations, teams, lifecycle
Description: Multi-tenant orgs/teams/facilities/memberships plus invite, provision, deactivate, role change, team removal, offboarding — no manual DB edits.
Acceptance: Full account lifecycle via API/UI; audited

Title: Retention policies
Description: Configurable data-retention/lifecycle rules by tenant, dataset, regulatory need, contract; applied by jobs; auditable.
Acceptance: Retention configurable, applied, audited

Title: Controlled deletion
Description: Verifiable deletion for data, users, orgs, offboarding with durable evidence of exactly what was deleted.
Acceptance: Deletion produces scope audit record; tested

Title: Backups & recovery
Description: Backup, restore, DR, recovery testing tied to retention; documented runbooks.
Acceptance: Test restore succeeds; procedure documented

Title: Data-governance audit trail
Description: Log access, edits, exports, deletions, policy/parser/monitoring changes, security events; queryable, protected, retained.
Acceptance: Security events logged and queryable

Title: Security foundation
Description: Encryption in transit & at rest, secrets management (no secrets in code, rotated), least privilege, defense in depth.
Acceptance: Encryption + secrets handling verified

Title: Compliance readiness (SOC2/ISO/GDPR)
Description: Map controls toward SOC 2 / ISO 27001 and GDPR right-to-erasure; produce control inventory + evidence plan (not certification).
Acceptance: Control inventory + evidence plan delivered

Title: Parser & field mapping
Description: Parse customer workbook (Tab 5 layout) and map each source field to a Sentry field with type and unit.
Acceptance: Representative log parses into tenant-scoped series

Title: Cadence detection [built]
Description: Infer each metric's intended sampling schedule from data — basis for missed vs never-scheduled. Built (detectCadence.js); integrate+test.
Acceptance: Cadence model per metric; unit-tested

Title: Correction stage [built]
Description: Fill short safe gaps & correct obvious errors using the cadence model; leave large gaps. Runs before analyzers. Built (correct.js); finish wiring.
Acceptance: Correction runs in-pipeline; over-fill guarded

Title: Augmentation (derived chemistry)
Description: Compute derived series (saturation ratios, indices, active-halogen) from raw readings. augment.js registry kept complete.
Acceptance: Augmenter registry complete; outputs match oracle

Title: Missing-data / validation stage
Description: Classify every gap (never-collected, stopped, started-late, frequent, inner/system pause, source gap) as a pure consumer of the cadence model; value validation (missing/future/dup/domain/limit/formula).
Acceptance: Findings match Sentry report categories

Title: Pipeline orchestration & contracts
Description: Wire parse->cadence->correct->augment->validate as ordered stages with clean hand-offs; analyzers get finished outputs only.
Acceptance: Stages independently testable; order enforced

Title: Validation & domain rules
Description: Reusable predicates: missing, future-date, duplicate, numeric/pH domain, lower/upper-limit breach, formula error.
Acceptance: Each rule unit-tested incl. edge cases

Title: SPC & capability math
Description: Control-chart stats (I-MR, X-bar/R, X-bar/S, EWMA, CUSUM, p, u), capability indices, distribution fits (normal/lognormal/Weibull). Engine behind graphs #6-15.
Acceptance: Statistics match reference values

Title: Scale & corrosion chemistry
Description: LSI/RSI/PSI/AI, mineral saturation panel, carbonate equilibrium (both directions), corrosion indicators. Total-analytical (documented).
Acceptance: Matches Proteus oracle (LSI -0.24; carbonate divergent pH ~6.86)

Title: Cooling-performance math
Description: Approach to wet bulb, condenser/evaporator approach, range, kW/ton, cost per ton-hour.
Acceptance: Validated against worked examples

Title: Water & chemical cost math
Description: RFQ 3D formulas: evap=circ*dT*0.00085; bleed=E/(N-1); makeup; water/sewer=gal/748*$/HCF; cycle savings; inhibitor loss; active-halogen. No invented savings.
Acceptance: Matches cost fixtures (~$18.2k example)

Title: Biological-control math
Description: Days-to-positive, ATP/plate-count handling, action-level logic.
Acceptance: Validated with SME

Title: Double-counting guards
Description: Cross-opportunity rules so cycles/reclaim/sewer savings use one consistent water balance & billing basis.
Acceptance: Combined-savings tests show no double-count

Title: Data-quality findings engine
Description: Produce report findings (source gaps, stopped logging, frequent gaps, inner/system pause, negative values, started late, never collected) with severity, affected metrics, what-to-do.
Acceptance: Findings match validated Sentry report

Title: Status & metrics rollup
Description: Per-metric status + metrics-by-status, problems-by-metrics-affected, issues-over-time summaries.
Acceptance: Rollups reconcile with findings

Title: Inhibitor/oxidant/corrosion/biological capability
Description: Four analyzers vs target/action levels.
Acceptance: Each flags known control failures in test data

Title: Heat-transfer & water-balance capability
Description: Approach/range/chilled-water (heat-transfer); makeup vs evap+bleed+drift residual (leak signal).
Acceptance: Detects seeded leak and fouling trend

Title: Chemical-feed capability
Description: Product use vs makeup/bleed/load/residual to flag over/underfeed, siphoning, pump miscalibration.
Acceptance: Flags seeded over/underfeed

Title: Operator/shift/before-after analysis
Description: Compare mean/spread/missingness by technician & weekday/shift; before-vs-after a change.
Acceptance: Differences surface with clear stats

Title: Regression & design-of-experiments
Description: Relate cycles/bleed/approach/chemical to load/wet-bulb/source/season; DOE plans. Feeds graphs #20/#23.
Acceptance: Regression feeds graphs; DOE plan documented

Title: Forecasting (statistical)
Description: Forecast water/chemical use, approach degradation, out-of-limit risk from the log. Feeds graph #46.
Acceptance: Forecast + band validated on held-out data

Title: Reusable graph framework
Description: Shared chart foundation: series binding from Sentry fields, target/limit/status bands, overlays (cleanings, chemical/source changes, calibration), filtering (date/asset/mode/technician/event), export/share. Every graph configures this.
Acceptance: Framework renders a sample graph with overlays + export

Title: Pontus /model endpoint

Acceptance: Matches prototype oracle / reproduces proteus-v7.html

Title: Model utility families
Description: utilities/chemical, /cost, /dynamics, /forecast — pure modules ported from the prototype oracle. See PROTEUS_PONTUS_SCOPE.md for full detail.
Acceptance: Matches prototype oracle / reproduces proteus-v7.html

Title: Proteus frontend
Description: Sentry-style vanilla ES-module client: makeup + tab-scoped controls, four tabs, charts; no chemistry in bundle. See PROTEUS_PONTUS_SCOPE.md for full detail.
Acceptance: Matches prototype oracle / reproduces proteus-v7.html

Title: Result caching
Description: Two-tier param-keyed cache (memory+localStorage, version-prefixed, rounded keys) + native fetch caching. See PROTEUS_PONTUS_SCOPE.md for full detail.
Acceptance: Matches prototype oracle / reproduces proteus-v7.html

Title: Decision workflow (accept/decline/defer/discuss)
Description: Per-opportunity Yes/No/Defer/Request-Discussion with record+audit; Yes triggers scheduling; No keeps available w/ reason; Defer sets review date; Discuss schedules without accepting.
Acceptance: All four paths record correctly and are audited

Title: Appointment setting
Description: On accept/discuss create scheduling path to Grant@ or Troy@YourWaterDr.com (pick Grant/Troy/next-available); collect contact+windows+tz+notes; attach summary, savings, graphs, status; failure -> visible error + email fallback; no private calendar exposure.
Acceptance: Test acceptance reaches correct contact with attachments; failure falls back to email

Title: Permissions & audit on actions
Description: Only authorized users can take/override decisions; decisions recorded immutably for Your Water Dr. review.
Acceptance: Unauthorized action blocked; decisions traceable

Title: Self-serve onboarding
Description: Facility onboarding, data-source connection, parser config, monitoring config, setup health checks, first-value path.
Acceptance: New facility onboards and sees first results unaided

Title: Ask Nereus integration
Description: Let Ask Nereus safely use governed Sentry data where permitted (tenant isolation + permissions).
Acceptance: Ask Nereus reads only permitted, tenant-scoped data

Title: Analytics/anomaly/alerting integration
Description: Make telemetry ingestion and monitoring outputs usable by analytics/anomaly/projection/alerting services.
Acceptance: Downstream services consume pipeline outputs

Title: Node/Express API surface
Description: Document+test routes behind onboarding, config, auth, retention, deletion, audit; production-ready.
Acceptance: Routes documented + test-covered

Title: DevOps & deployment
Description: Cloud deploy, CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, secure deployment, operational docs.
Acceptance: Repeatable, secure deploy path documented

Title: Test & QA package

Acceptance: Acceptance test package passes; errors user-readable

Acceptance: Docs complete enough for future maintenance
